summary: Clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1988 show with standout Sugaree, Desolation Row and an electrifying Mexicali Blues solo.
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source that delivers clear instrument separation, though occasional gurgles are noted. The performance bursts with energy – the first set shines on Sugaree, Desolation Row and Deal, while the second set builds momentum through Crazy Fingers, Play‑in/Uncle and a blistering Mexicali Blues solo. Overall the concert is praised as a strong example of the band’s late‑80s power despite a few uneven moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑star ratings and highlight both excellent soundboard quality and high musical energy; only a handful of criticisms (vocals, isolated song slips) keep the score just above a solid ‘good’ rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced sound; reviewers note clear vocals and instruments. Minor issues include occasional gurgles on “Cassidy” and a brief interruption in the first set, but no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are short but packed with energy; the first set delivers strong, concise performances while the second set maintains focus and builds intensity, especially after a high‑octane “Crazy Fingers” → “Playin/Uncle” sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring guitar on Sugaree and an extended solo on Mexicali Blues; some reviewers note vocal strain on Crazy Fingers and a cracked voice during the encore.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for his spoken “Phil Speaks” moment and solid bass work on Desolation Row; listeners can hear his line clearly in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are described as clear and present throughout, though one reviewer wonders about occasional trash‑can sounds during the jam.
